blocks.text: lay awake until past midnight. He never stopped watching the window.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You look like you reasoned with yourself for eight hours.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Julian. Whatever you're thinking — we don't have enough to think it.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: These are people on a weekend trip. An argument happens and someone walks past a window at night and you are already building a case that doesn't exist.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You're the most honest person I know except about this.
I didn't have an answer for that yet.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Breakfast. Then the beach. Then we leave tomorrow afternoon and none of this is anything.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Say it like you mean it.

options.label: He's trying to. He genuinely is.
No. And Mara already knows that.
